name: debugtools-timer-freeze-modern-debug
description: Issue #11 slice 1 review-fix regression scenario for the title-idle-timer freeze fix: opens the debug hub once via the SELECT+R hotkey (600-606) and then holds it open with no further input across the vanilla `proc->timer_idle == 815` attract-mode-transition threshold (which a plain, hub-free run reaches around frame 1292 -- see probe-derived offset proc->timer_idle == frame - 477 while running). gDebugToolsProbe.titleIdleTimerSample (Title_IDLE's DebugTools_RecordTitleIdleTimer call, include/expansion_debugtools.h) is asserted byte-for-byte IDENTICAL at frame 620 (shortly after the hub opens) and again at frame 1290 (670 frames later, well past the 815 threshold) -- proving proc->timer_idle does not advance at all while the hub remains active, so the exact `== 815` equality is never silently skipped past. The hub is then closed via a B press (1300-1306, MenuCancelSelect's MENU_ACT_END path -- see include/uimenu.h), and a final checkpoint at frame 1450 asserts titleIdleTimerSample has resumed increasing (strictly greater than the frozen value), proving idle-timer progress resumes correctly once the hub closes rather than staying stuck forever. hubOpenCount is also probed at every checkpoint and must stay exactly 1 throughout (opening once, holding, and closing must never spawn or require a second hub). No release counterpart is needed: DebugTools_IsHubActive() is compiled out to a constant 0 in a release build (see docs/debugtools.md), so the freeze branch in Title_IDLE is provably dead code there and the existing debugtools-hub-modern-release.json already demonstrates gDebugToolsProbe stays all-zero throughout an identical-length input window.
